purpose: Freeze a reproducible RECTANGLE-80 input difference before RuntimeE4 topology attribution.

queries.query: RECTANGLE block cipher 16-round differential characteristic input difference output difference 2^-62.13

results.title: RECTANGLE: A Bit-slice Lightweight Block Cipher Suitable for Multiple Platforms
results.finding: Confirms the 16-round propagation probability and the best-trail weights, but does not publish a concrete input difference in the NIST copy.

results.title: RECTANGLE: A Bit-slice Lightweight Block Cipher Suitable for Multiple Platforms
results.finding: Canonical ePrint PDF; indexed excerpts mention the same probability results and a 14-round propagation appendix.

results.title: RECTANGLE: A Bit-slice Ultra-Lightweight Block Cipher
results.finding: Confirms the 4 x 16 state, row rotations (0, 1, 12, 13), and full dependency after four rounds.

verified_sources.title: RECTANGLE: A Bit-slice Lightweight Block Cipher Suitable for Multiple Platforms
verified_facts: The best six-round single differential trail has weight 18 according to the design paper table.

verified_sources.title: Improving the Search Algorithm for the Best Differential/Linear Trails of Bit-Permutation-Based Ciphers - Supplementary Information
verified_facts: The first-round input has cell 14 difference 0x6 and cell 3 difference 0x5 in the artifact's internal cell order.
The source uses the final revised RECTANGLE S-box and row rotations (0,1,12,13).

mapping.project_physical_bit_rule: bit index = 16 * row + column; nibble bit row is the bit value used by rectangle_sub_columns
mapping.mapping_status: verified against the repository print loop, final S-box table, and project ShiftRow implementation

decision.warning: This freezes data construction only. It is not evidence that RuntimeE4 uses the correct topology; that requires correct/corrupted/no-topology controls.
